content:
Which organ in the human body is primarily responsible for pumping blood?

A. Lung
B. Liver
C. Heart
D. Kidney

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

What is the capital city of Japan?

A. Seoul
B. Tokyo
C. Beijing
D. Bangkok

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which planet is closest to the Sun?

A. Venus
B. Mercury
C. Mars
D. Earth

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which substance do plants use from the air during photosynthesis?

A. Oxygen
B. Nitrogen
C. Carbon dioxide
D. Hydrogen

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

Which country invented paper?

A. Egypt
B. China
C. Greece
D. India

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which mathematical operation is the inverse of multiplication?

A. Addition
B. Subtraction
C. Division
D. Exponentiation

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

Which ocean is the largest on Earth?

A. Atlantic Ocean
B. Pacific Ocean
C. Indian Ocean
D. Southern Ocean

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which metal is liquid at room temperature?

A. Mercury
B. Aluminum
C. Iron
D. Copper

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
A

Which type of rock forms from cooled lava or magma?

A. Sedimentary
B. Metamorphic
C. Igneous
D. Fossil

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

Which instrument is used to observe distant stars and galaxies?

A. Microscope
B. Telescope
C. Periscope
D. Spectrometer

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which vitamin is mainly obtained from sunlight exposure?

A. Vitamin A
B. Vitamin B12
C. Vitamin C
D. Vitamin D

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
D

Which part of a plant conducts photosynthesis?

A. Roots
B. Leaves
C. Stem
D. Flowers

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which layer of Earth is the outermost?

A. Mantle
B. Core
C. Crust
D. Lithosphere

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

Which organ helps humans breathe by exchanging oxygen and carbon dioxide?

A. Heart
B. Lungs
C. Stomach
D. Brain

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which scientist discovered penicillin?

A. Alexander Fleming
B. Louis Pasteur
C. Robert Hooke
D. Gregor Mendel

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
A

Which type of energy does a moving object have?

A. Potential energy
B. Chemical energy
C. Kinetic energy
D. Nuclear energy

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

Which country has the largest population in the world?

A. India
B. United States
C. China
D. Indonesia

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
A

Which gas is most responsible for the greenhouse effect?

A. Oxygen
B. Nitrogen
C. Carbon dioxide
D. Neon

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

Which animal is known as the fastest land animal?

A. Lion
B. Horse
C. Cheetah
D. Leopard

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

Which branch of mathematics deals with shapes and their properties?

A. Algebra
B. Geometry
C. Calculus
D. Statistics

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which natural satellite orbits Earth?

A. Mars
B. The Moon
C. Europa
D. Titan

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which device stores electrical energy chemically and releases it as electricity?

A. Capacitor
B. Generator
C. Battery
D. Motor

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

Which type of cloud is typically associated with thunderstorms?

A. Cirrus
B. Cumulonimbus
C. Stratus
D. Altocumulus

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which number system uses only the digits 0 and 1?

A. Decimal
B. Binary
C. Octal
D. Hexadecimal

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which blood cells help fight infections?

A. Red blood cells
B. White blood cells
C. Platelets
D. Plasma

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which continent contains the Amazon Rainforest?

A. Africa
B. Asia
C. South America
D. Europe

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C

Which scientist proposed the heliocentric model where Earth orbits the Sun?

A. Galileo Galilei
B. Nicolaus Copernicus
C. Johannes Kepler
D. Tycho Brahe

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
B

Which unit measures the intensity of earthquakes?

A. Richter scale
B. Kelvin scale
C. Pascal scale
D. Joule scale

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
A

Which organelle is responsible for producing energy in cells?

A. Mitochondria
B. Ribosome
C. Nucleus
D. Golgi apparatus

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
A

Which physical quantity is measured in Newtons?

A. Energy
B. Power
C. Force
D. Speed

Respond only with the letter of the correct answer.
C
A student buys 3 notebooks for $4 each and a pen for $2. How much does the student spend in total?
A bus travels 60 kilometers per hour for 5 hours. How far does the bus travel?
There are 96 cookies that need to be divided equally among 8 children. How many cookies does each child receive?
A store had 200 apples. If 75 apples were sold and 40 more were delivered, how many apples are in the store now?
A rope is 48 meters long. It is cut into pieces of 6 meters each. How many pieces are made?
Lina reads 18 pages of a book each day. How many pages will she read in 7 days?
A farmer plants 9 rows of trees with 11 trees in each row. How many trees are planted in total?
A water tank holds 500 liters. If 135 liters are used, how much water remains?
A movie lasts 2 hours and 30 minutes. How many minutes long is the movie?
A class collects 15 cans per day for recycling. How many cans will they collect in 12 days?
